blurb: Plump, boat-shaped leaves in loose rosettes that warm to gold and amber-orange with sunset-red tips in strong sun. Branches and clumps freely into a glowing little mound.

care.light: Lots of light — including direct sun — is what turns it gold and orange; in shade it reverts to plain green and the rosettes open and stretch. Acclimate before any big jump in sun.
care.water: Soak-and-dry; the fleshy leaves store plenty, so wait until they soften slightly and the soil is bone dry.
care.temperature: Room temperature; keep above ~30°F and bring in before frost.
care.dormancy: A warm-season grower that slows in deep winter — keep it bright and drier then.
care.feeding: A dilute feed once or twice through spring and summer.
care.potting: Repot every couple of years; pinch back leggy stems to keep it full.
care.propagation: Very easy from dropped leaves and stem cuttings laid on dry mix.
care.bloom: Clusters of small, star-shaped white flowers in the warmer months (spring into summer).
care.troubles: Overwatering rot and etiolation (stretching, colour loss) in low light; mealybugs.
care.toxicity: Sedum is a pet-safe genus — the ASPCA lists Sedum relatives such as burro's tail (S. morganianum) as non-toxic to cats and dogs (and horses).
